properties.description: Classical Chinese garden from the Ming Dynasty (1559), featuring pavilions, rockeries, and koi ponds
properties.notes: Arrive early to avoid crowds. The surrounding bazaar area is free to explore.

properties.description: One of China's finest museums, with 11 permanent galleries spanning ancient bronzes, ceramics, calligraphy, jade, and Ming-Qing furniture across a striking circular building on People's Square
properties.notes: Free admission but timed-entry tickets required — book online in advance via the official WeChat channel or website. English audio guide available. The bronze gallery and ancient ceramics collection are highlights.

properties.description: Active Buddhist temple founded in 1882, housing two prized jade Buddha statues brought from Burma — a serene island of calm in a dense urban neighborhood
properties.notes: The reclining jade Buddha on the upper floor is the more impressive of the two. Respectful dress required (no shorts/sleeveless). The temple vegetarian restaurant is popular at lunch (open 11:00-14:00).

properties.description: Moving museum in the historic Ohel Moshe Synagogue documenting the 20,000+ Jewish refugees who found sanctuary in Shanghai's Hongkou district during WWII (1933-1945)
properties.notes: Deeply affecting exhibit with personal stories, photographs, and artifacts. The surrounding Tilanqiao area still has many original 1930s-40s buildings from the Jewish ghetto. Allow extra time to walk the neighborhood.

properties.description: Basement museum in the French Concession housing a private collection of over 3,000 original Chinese propaganda posters from the 1950s-1980s — one of Shanghai's most unusual museums
properties.address: Basement, Building B, 868 Huashan Rd, Xuhui District
properties.notes: Easy to miss — look for the small sign in the residential compound. Run by a passionate private collector. High-quality reproduction prints available to buy. Combine with French Concession walking tour nearby.

properties.description: Shanghai's most atmospheric neighborhood — tree-lined avenues of Art Deco villas, boutique cafes, independent bookshops, and remnants of 1920s-40s colonial-era architecture
properties.address: Wukang Rd / Fuxing Rd area, Xuhui District
properties.sustainability_notes: Many historic heritage buildings preserved; walkable neighborhood that rewards foot exploration over taxi
properties.notes: Start at Wukang Mansion (武康大楼) at the tip of Wukang Rd — the iconic bow-fronted building is a beloved photo spot. Walk to Fuxing Park, Xintiandi, and Tianzifang. Best explored on foot or by bike. Weekend afternoons are lively.

properties.description: China's first state-run contemporary art museum, occupying a converted 1985 power plant with a chimney repurposed as a public observation tower — hosts the Shanghai Biennale
properties.sustainability_notes: Industrial heritage building given new life through adaptive reuse; champions sustainable urban renewal
properties.notes: Free admission. The industrial architecture is as impressive as the art. The 165m chimney lookout (open on weekends) offers a different perspective of the city. Located on the South Bund waterfront — combine with a riverfront walk.
